message:
fix(dtype,iter): prevent signed→unsigned same_kind cast and fix negative stride classification

Dtype casting (fnp-dtype):
- Prevent signed integer → unsigned integer casts under same_kind policy
  (e.g. I32→U8 now correctly rejected) to match NumPy's type hierarchy
  where signed integers should only cast to other signed integers, floats,
  or complex types within the same kind
- Add test assertion for I32→U8 same_kind rejection

Transfer loop (fnp-iter):
- Fix overlap_copy_policy to return NoCopy for zero-length transfers
  instead of erroring — zero-length copies are valid no-ops
- Fix contiguity detection in select_transfer_loop: use signed stride
  comparison (== item_size as isize) instead of unsigned_abs() to prevent
  negative strides from being classified as contiguous
- Negative unit strides now correctly classify as StridedNoCast/
  StridedWithCast instead of SimpleContiguous/ContiguousCast since
  they require reverse iteration, not a simple memcpy
- Widen count_true_mask chunk size from 8 to 128 for better cache
  line utilization with simpler branch-based counting
- Add test for identity overlap (src==dst) returning ForwardCopy
- Update negative stride test expectations and comments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
